title.rendered: We Are Talking Christmas… But for A Very Good Reason

content.rendered:
Is it too early to mention the C word? CHRISTMAS! Well, it isn’t too early to talk about planning a magical Christmas market getaway.

Christmas markets started in Germany hundreds of years ago. The very first one was recorded in Dresden way back in 1434. Now, Christmas markets can be found in many magical locations across Europe.

Here is our guide to some of the most beautiful and magical Christmas markets that you could visit this Christmas across Europe, and most of them are not obvious choices.

Have you ever heard of Montbéliard in France? The streets of this beautiful French town are lit up and transformed into a Christmas wonderland with 140 stalls in this very authentic German Christmas market. The town, in the East, is famous around the world for its enchanting festive illuminations and is classed as one of the most beautiful markets in France.

Trier in Germany has been hosting this traditional Christmas market for 43 years. The 95 stalls are set up in front of the spectacular backdrop of the Trier Cathedral. The entertainment schedule changes daily and visitors can enjoy professional singers, dancing and a historical puppet theatre. Trier is just 30 minutes from Luxembourg airport. Or, what about flying into Cologne. It is around 2.5 hours away. You can spend a day visiting the famous Cologne Christmas markets, before heading to Trier. Now, that would be a magical and very festive weekend away!

I am not sure we would usually think of Hungary or Estonia having top 20 ranking Christmas markets, would we? But both the Budapest and Tallinn markets are up there as some of the most magical Christmas markets in Europe.

The Budapest Advent Feast is set in front of the stunning Basilica and last year it won the best Christmas market for the second time. Every year there is a spectacular light show off the façade of the Basilica, which is the backdrop to the many artisan and gastronomic stalls.

Tallinn is transformed into a winter wonderland with many stalls and Santa’s house surrounded by its grand and historical buildings. More than half a million people flock to this Christmas market every single year. It was voted the best Christmas market in Europe three years ago.

I don’t think we would normally consider Italy as a place to go for a Christmas market. Think again! Govone, which is just over an hour from Turin, is a unique Christmas experience. Set in the hills of the Lange-Roero, which is the wine region of Piedmont, you will find the little town of Govone or the winter wonderland of Italy. There is street food, you will find fine wines, stalls with local delicacies and a special Santa Claus musical show in the very festive Savoy Royal Castle every day. If you like Italy and you like Christmas, then this is one to consider.

If you fancy some winter sun, what about Christmas markets on the islands of Madeira and Porto Santo? You can get the ferry between the two, it takes about two and a half hours, and enjoy the Christmas festivities in both of these beautiful Portuguese destinations. The islands are lit up in their Christmas splendour from 1st December through to the 9th January.

If you are travelling to Europe for some Christmas shopping, then don’t forget to order your travel money and get it delivered safe and securely directly to your door. No matter which country you are visiting, your trusted currency supplier Travel FX has you covered for your Euros at the very best rates.

@graph.name: Lucy Reed
@graph.description: Hi! I’m Lucy, as well as writing blog posts I lead Customer Experience and UX at Travel FX. With over 15 years’ experience in the foreign exchange industry, I specialise in helping UK travellers understand exchange rates, avoid unnecessary fees, and feel confident about their travel money before they fly. Having lived both in the UK and overseas, and travelled extensively over the years, I understand first-hand how important it is to get the details right when you’re planning a trip. From managing your travel budget to choosing the right foreign currency or travel money card, small decisions can make a big difference.
